findings.message: Version floor mismatch between production and dev: production allows 'httpx>=0.27' while [dependency-groups].dev already pins 'httpx>=0.28'. This means production could resolve to an older minor than what is exercised in tests, undermining parity.
findings.suggestion: Raise the production floor to match dev: "httpx>=0.28,<1.0" (or unify both to a single floor).

findings.message: 'httpx' is already declared in [dependency-groups].dev. Adding it to the production [project].dependencies is only justified if production application code (not just tests or TestClient) makes HTTP calls. The diff contains no accompanying source change to confirm a production use-case.
findings.suggestion: Verify a production code path actually imports httpx before promoting it out of dev. If it is only used via FastAPI's TestClient or similar, remove it from [project].dependencies and keep it only in dev.

findings.message: The '<1.0' upper bound is conservative and correct today (httpx is pre-1.0 as of 0.2x), but will silently block upgrades when httpx 1.0 ships and may cause resolver conflicts downstream.
findings.suggestion: Track the httpx 1.0 release; plan a coordinated bump of the upper bound at that time. Consider adding a TODO comment or a Dependabot/Renovate alert rule.

findings.message: Supply-chain note (informational): httpx (encode/httpx, Tom Christie) is actively maintained, widely adopted, and has a clean release history. No elevated supply-chain risk identified.
findings.suggestion: No action required; flagged for completeness.
